title: Lazy Sunday Brie Fondue
description: A spicy and velvety brie fondue for lazy Sundays, inspired by Syrian cuisine. Raw-friendly and perfect for snacking.

ingredients.name: 4 slices of creamy brie
ingredients.notes: Ensure the cheese is at room temperature.

ingredients.name: 1 tablespoon red pepper flakes
ingredients.notes: Adjust to taste for spiciness level.

ingredients.name: 2 tablespoons lemon juice
ingredients.notes: Can be omitted for milder version.

ingredients.name: 1 cup heavy cream
ingredients.notes: Use fresh and pasteurized if possible.

instructions: Place the brie slices on a baking sheet at room temperature or in the refrigerator to soften.
In a medium saucepan, heat heavy cream over medium heat until just before it starts to simmer. Stir in lemon juice if using.
Once the cream is hot but not boiling, add red pepper flakes and garlic; stir well to combine. Allow the mixture to come back to a gentle simmer for 1-2 minutes to allow the flavors to meld together.
Remove from heat. Slice the brie into bite-sized pieces and place them in the bowl of your fondue pot or another suitable container for serving.
Pour the hot cream mixture over the top of the sliced brie cheese, ensuring all slices are covered with a smooth layer of the sauce.
Serve with crusty bread, vegetables (like cherry tomatoes and bell peppers), or any other dipping items. Enjoy your lazy Sunday fondue experience!
